Headline: Elevated Sediment Fuels in Rotterdam
Content: FOBAS has tested numbers of samples from Rotterdam and found out they exceeded the specified Total Sediment Potential (TSP) per ISO 8217 limits. The TSP results of these samples range from 0.17-0.26% mass, whilst other fuel parameters indicated satisfactory results.

Headline: Australia to Quarantine Vessels from China
Content:
Due to fears emerging from the spread of the Coronavirus, Australia has said that vessels coming from China after 1st February will be quarantined for 14 days. Such action could lead to costly delays for scheduled carrier services.

Source: https://lloydslist.maritimeintelligence.informa.com/LL1130877/Coronavirus-Australia-to-quarantine-vessels-from-China

Headline: Unipec Halts West African Crude Oil Buying
Content:
China's Unipec has halted purchases of West African oil due to the slow oil demand caused by the Coronavirus outbreak.

Source: https://af.reuters.com/article/energyOilNews/idAFL8N2A358M

Headline: Hapag - Lloyd to Lower CO2 Emissions Using Biofuel
Content:
Container line Hapag-Lloyd has said it will start using a blend of biofuel in order to reduce CO2 emissions. The company has fuelled a vessel at Rotterdam with B20, a blend of 80% low sulfur fuel oil and 20% biofuel.

Source: https://shipandbunker.com/news/emea/772649-hapag-lloyd-announces-biofuel-trial-to-cut-carbon-emissions

Headline: Eastern Pacific Shipping Reports See 4.6% Drop in CO2 Emissions
Content:
Eastern Pacific Shipping reported that its carbon emissions dropped to 10.3 g/tonne mile last year, using the energy efficiency operational indicator formula.

Source: https://shipandbunker.com/news/apac/208650-eastern-pacific-shipping-reports-46-drop-in-carbon-emissions-in-2019
